page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz, the instance features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled to deliver 2 threads per core. The CPU architecture is x86_64, utilizing a dedicated allocation model. Cache resources include 32 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 33 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the instance delivers average single-core capabilities but exhibits limitations under multi-threaded workloads. Single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests place the instance in the average middle tier, supported by hardware instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. However, multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and ray tracing benchmarks fall into the lower 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached operations also register in the lower quartile, though Passmark memory scores remain average.

The n2-standard-4 offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads that do not demand extreme parallel processing or high-throughput memory access. Due to its dedicated CPU allocation, it provides more predictable performance than burstable instances, representing a stable baseline for standard applications. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and lower multi-core efficiency are notable tradeoffs for database-heavy or high-performance computing tasks. It is best suited for moderate web serving, text processing, and basic application hosting.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor at 2.8 GHz. The instance provides dedicated CPU allocation with 2 physical cores and hyper-threading, supported by 33 MB of L3 cache and AVX-512 instruction sets. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU and text processing performance, but multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the lower performance tiers. This configuration is best suited for general-purpose workloads, moderate web serving, and basic application hosting where predictable, non-burstable CPU performance is required without heavy multi-threaded or storage-intensive demands.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering stable single-core performance for standard application hosting.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-4 is a general-purpose VM with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing cores and a standard one-to-four CPU-to-memory ratio.
bullet_points: Features 4 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM on a dedicated Intel Xeon processor
Delivers average single-core CPU and text processing performance
Includes AVX-512 instruction set support for vector calculations
Lacks bundled local storage and GPU hardware accelerators
Best suited for moderate web serving and general-purpose application hosting
